understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each KA59 level by moving and positioning all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a `+` in the semantic grid is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, producing a WIN for the level and ultimately completing all levels.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, updating its pixel-coordinate position (e.g., [x,y] -> [x±3,y] or [x,y±3]) unless blocked by a wall/boundary or other blocking interaction. Some adjacent objects can be pushable, so moving into them may push them if space/behavior allows, while walls generally prevent movement though their blocking behavior may vary by level. When multiple selectables exist, control can be switched using CLICK with the exact pixel coordinates of another selectable; subsequent MOVE actions apply only to the newly selected piece.
